mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in the residential real estate sector, driving positive equity returns through wealth effects and construction spending over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns (win rate < 50%) or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects deteriorating manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain bottlenecks, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.03 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 205, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in > 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward testing (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real sales of existing homes confirms broad-based demand expansion across production and consumption channels, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined positive momentum of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns (win rate < 55%) or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, a rising unemployment rate indicates that labor market normalization is stalling, which suppresses consumer confidence and corporate hiring plans, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases during post-shock recovery periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the regime-specific predictive power (Rank IC) falls below 0.03.
